topic: Someone receives flowers with no card attached
raw_output:
[story 1]

The delivery arrived just as I was about to settle in for the evening. A large, slightly wilted bouquet of sunflowers sat on the doorstep, vibrant yellow mocking the dull grey of the afternoon. I snatched the arrangement, the stems cool and slightly damp beneath my fingers, and immediately turned to inspect the wrapping. Nothing. Just the flowers, a silent, unsolicited offering. "Seriously?" I muttered, the sound sharp enough to echo in the quiet hallway. I examined the petals, searching for any clue, any indication of who had taken the time to select them, but there was only the blank space where a note should have been. I tossed the bundle onto the counter with a frustrated sigh, already mentally calculating how long it would take to find a proper message, or perhaps just to get rid of them.

[story 2]

My eyes immediately locked onto the floral display when the doorbell chimed. A cascade of roses, deep crimson and impossibly perfect, was waiting for me. I didn't bother to check the caller ID; the anticipation of the delivery was already tightening my chest. I took the heavy vase, my grip firm, and carried it into the living room. I circled the arrangement twice, my gaze sweeping over the pristine blooms, then darting to the empty space beside them. My jaw tightened. I leaned in close to the stems, scrutinizing the wrapping paper again, my brow furrowed in deep concentration, expecting some hidden inscription, some explanation for this grand gesture. When my fingers found only smooth paper, a low, irritated sound escaped my throat. I set the vase down with a decisive clunk, already planning to discard the entire thing.

[story 3]

When the courier handed over the package, I felt a distinct surge of annoyance bubble up. It was a simple arrangement of lilies, undeniably beautiful, yet completely devoid of context. I took the box, my movements jerky, and carried it straight to the kitchen. I ripped open the packaging, my fingers brushing against the cool silk of the wrapping. I scanned the interior, my eyes darting around, searching for the expected paper. Nothing. Just the flowers, standing stiffly in their cellophane. I let out a sharp exhale, a sound laced with unmistakable exasperation. "Are you kidding me?" I whispered to the empty room, already picturing the tedious task of finding out who this was, and why they hadn't bothered with a single line of explanation.
